label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to work through this question. The question is asking why the text uses a lot of figures. The options are A, B, C, or D. First, I need to understand the text. The passage is about the growth of credit card usage in China and the implications of that growth. The author mentions numbers like "about 30 percent of Chinese urban households now own at least one credit card", "an average of 40 percent per year between 2004 and 2009", "90 percent of non-credit card users were unaware...", "72 percent...were never late on their payments", and so on. The question is asking what the purpose of including these figures is. Let's look at the options again: A) record the valuable data B) provide concrete evidence C) introduce a research method D) give a detailed description Option A: "record the valuable data" – This might be a possible answer because the text is presenting data. But the question is about the purpose of using the figures, not just recording them. The text uses these numbers to support points being made by the researcher, not just to record for the sake of it. Option B: "provide concrete evidence" – This seems likely. The figures are used to back up the claims made by Rui Yao. For example, when she says that lack of knowledge could create problems, she supports that with the 90% and 60% figures. The numbers provide evidence for the arguments being made in the text. Option C: "introduce a research method" – The text doesn't explain how the research was conducted. It doesn't mention things like sample size, surveys, or other methods. The figures are presented as results, not as part of explaining the method. So this is probably not the right answer. Option D: "give a detailed description" – While the figures do add details, the purpose of those details is to support the argument rather than just to describe something in detail. The main point of the text is to discuss the implications of credit card usage growth in China, and the figures are used as evidence for those implications. So maybe B is more accurate. Let me check the text again to see if there's a clue. The first paragraph mentions the growth rate of credit card adoption, and then in the following paragraphs, the researcher uses these statistics to highlight both positive and negative aspects. For example, the 90% of non-users unaware of safety issues is presented to show a problem with knowledge. Similarly, the 70% who said credit cards led to overspending supports the negative implications. So the figures are used
